About Projects Blog

Ohmmi

November, 2016

"Ohmmi is a no frills meditation timer that gives the users a pleasant and simple experience for practicing mindful meditation."

This project was my answer to having forgotten the login password of a meditation app that I used at the time. Wanting to create a simple experience that gets right to the point, I set out to build an android app that I would enjoy using.

This was also a great deep dive with React-Native, Android development, and the Play Store publishing process.

I published an article on the process of creating this app, you can read more here.

The app is available on the Google Play Store

USED

timyanchenwang.com

August, 2016

Revamping my personal site with github pages; using jekyll and, the very aptly named theme, "hyde".

The bulk of the work involved taking the "hyde" theme and pulling it apart while altering some of the ruby based jekyll components.

USED

tweetcost.com

September, 2015

A web app experiment to keep track of my personal finances by openly sharing it with the world. Tweetcost was a single-page-app that featured a large counter which would update every 2 minutes.

The idea was that I would tweet out the prices of my expenses as I traveled and meteor would query Twitter's api every 2 minutes crawling through any new posts made by me since the last scan.

Any new tweets with prices included and a designated hashtag would be returned as JSON and parsed by the app and stored in the backend. The front end would total the new cost and display it.

USED
